content.claim: Between-school variance in Hungary is the third highest among OECD countries, while within-school variance is below OECD average, indicating inequality driven by school segregation rather than within-classroom sorting.
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Does not directly prove that 6/8-year gymnázium tracks are the primary driver of between-school variance
Could reflect other institutional factors (fee structures, location, language specialization) bundled with academic selection
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
